title: Expression vectors from "Description of a novel extremophile green algae, Chlamydomonas pacifica, and its potential as a biotechnology host."
author: Molino, João Vitor
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Cytosolic vectors for Chlamydomonas reinhardtii 
 pAH04 mCherry (5536 bp)  (Molino et al., 2018)  
 
 
 AmpR (Ampicillin Resistance): 
 
 
 Positioned at approximately 4000 bp, this gene confers resistance to ampicillin, enabling bacterial selection. 
 
 
 Amp Promoter: 
 
 
 Regulates the expression of the AmpR gene, facilitating its transcription and subsequent resistance to ampicillin. 
 
 
 f1 ori: 
 
 
 Positioned near 5000 bp, this origin of replication (ori) facilitates plasmid  replication in bacterial cells, ensuring propagation of the plasmid in E. coli. 
 
 
 PAR1 - HSP70 Promoter + RBCS2 Promoter: 
 
 
 A dual-promoter system that combines the heat-shock protein 70 (HSP70) and ribulose bisphosphate carboxylase small subunit 2 (RBCS2) promoters, providing strong and constitutive expression of downstream genes in Chlamydomonas. 
 
 
 mCherry: 
 
 
 Encodes the mCherry fluorescent protein, a red fluorescent marker commonly used for visualizing gene expression in vivo. 
 
 
 Ble (Bleomycin Resistance): 
 
 
 Provides resistance to bleomycin 
 
 
 Intron and UTRs (rbcS2): 
 
 
 Includes untranslated regions (UTRs) and introns from the rbcS2 gene, enhancing gene expression stability and processing in Chlamydomonas reinhardtii. 
 
 
 XbaI (93 bp) and KpnI (2750 bp): 
 
 
 These are key restriction enzyme sites used for cloning and linearization of the plasmid. 
   
 Secretion vectors for  Chlamydomonas pacifica  
 pJPSHx1_PHL7 (5879 bp)  (Molino, Oliver, et al., 2024)  
 
 
 AmpR (Ampicillin Resistance): 
 
 
 Enables bacterial selection by conferring resistance to ampicillin. 
 
 
 Amp Promoter: 
 
 
 Drives the transcription of the AmpR gene, allowing for the maintenance of plasmids in ampicillin-containing media. 
 
 
 P_TUBA2 Promoter: 
 
 
 Promoter from the tubulin alpha-2 gene from C. pacifica that controls expression of downstream genes, including PHL7, in eukaryotic cells. 
 
 
 T_TUBA2 
 
 
 Terminator region of tubulin alpha-2 gene from C. pacifica 
 
 
 PHL7: 
 
 
 Encodes the PHL7 enzyme, a plastic-degrading enzyme, important for applications in biodegradation and recycling. 
 
 
 F2A Peptide: 
 
 
 A viral peptide sequence that allows for the co-expression and separation of proteins from a single mRNA transcript. 
 
 
 SP7 (Signal Peptide 7): 
 
 
 Directs the PHL7 protein to the secretory pathway for export from the cell. 
 
 
 Step Hygro aph7'' (Hygromycin Resistance): 
 
 
 Provides resistance to hygromycin, facilitating selection of transformed eukaryotic cells. 
 
 
 XbaI (56 bp) and KpnI (3056 bp): 
 
 
 These are key restriction enzyme sites used for cloning and linearization of the plasmid.
